query_text: Please advise what kind of skin disease is this. Middle aged male.  No history of allergy on food.  Recurrent on the back of the neck.  What disease is it?  What kinds of medicine is needed?  Can it be cured?
gold_texts: ["Psoriasis.  Use orally Psoriasis capsules, glycyrrhizic acid glycosides, transfer factors.  Topically, use Momosone, tacrolimus.", "Psoriasis. .....", "Psoriasis Vulgaris.  Use chinese medicine for treatment.", "Looks like Psoriasis.", "Psoriasis Vulgaris", "Should be Psoriasis.", "kerion in chinese medicine.", "Psoriasis Vulgaris.  Is a genetic disease with multiple genes.  Prescribing Cabotriol, halomethasone for topical use.  Orally take Ke Yin Wan (A Chinese herbal drug), compound oxalate glycoside.  Prevent from infections (bacteria, virus, fungi).  Prevent from injury (bath with water below 40 degree C).  Getting Koebner Phenomenon.  Avoid consumption of alcohol.  Avoid drug of beta receptors blockers and NSAID drugs.", "Tinea.  Caused by fungal infection. Recommendation: 1.        Topical application of clotrimazole ointment, Miao natural ointment, Dakonin ointment or clotrimazole tinea solution.  Treatment time: 2 – 4 weeks. 2.      Orally use Ketoconazole or Griseofulvin.  Treatment time: 2 – 4 weeks.  Should supplement that with anti-inflammatory drugs.  As the infection could involve many sources,  use drugs such as floxacin, azithromycin, clindamycin, etc.  If having beri beri, should be dealt with timely to avoid cross infections.  Should use vitamins C, AD, E and B types vitamins, with small amount of zinc, iron, calcium etc.  3.      Avoid contact with outside  ** material and locally **.  Don’t scratch, don’t rub with force, don’t use hot water and soap when bathing.  Don’t consume alcohol, don’t drink coffee, don’t drink tea, don’t eat something sour, spicy or other ** food. 4.      Locally poke the needle knife point to dredge open the blood vessels (This is Chinese Acupuncture Medicine)."]

As the infection could involve many sources,  use drugs such as floxacin, azithromycin, clindamycin, etc.  If having beri beri, should be dealt with timely to avoid cross infections. candidate: Likely irritant contact dermatitis.  Moisturize, avoid irritants, consider topical steroids.  See dermatologist if persists.
query_text: See picture for the disease Skin lesions on a 3 years old infant.  Not itchy.
gold_texts: ["frictional lichenoid rash", "Could be frictional lichenoid rash or lichen striatus", "frictional lichenoid rash", "frictional lichenoid rash", "frictional lichenoid rash", "Also called sandy dermatitis or juvenile papular dermatitis.  It is the kind of skin disease commonly taking place in summer and autumn for pre-school children.  Easily seen in summer when children are playing outdoor.  More happening in boys than girls. Some people think that is related to  mechanical contact with exposure to sun.", "frictional lichenoid rash", "Sandy dermatitis", "Sandy dermatitis"]

candidate: Likely chronic hand eczema, possibly irritant contact dermatitis. Patch testing recommended.  Avoid irritants.  See dermatologist.
query_text: Related to Eczema, pompholyx.  Request for diagnosis. Suffering eczema and epompholyx at hands from the year of 2010.  It is attacking me recurrently.  Very painful.  Hope that physicians from all places could take a look.  Following is the history:-  Work History:  Graduated in June, 2009.  Start working after that.  Work requires contact with  hydrochloric acid with low concentration inside a trench.  It is used to clean the oxidized surface of metal.  There is no use of protective gloves durig the process, and has direct contact with the hydrochloric acid.  Both hands are involved in the physical work, have direct contact with industrial grease.  Change of work place in March 2010.  Working in the office there, with occasional contact with metal.  Not sure it is caused by allergy with metal.  Symptoms:  Blisters on the sides of both palms.  Very itchy.  If broken after scratching, liquid of light yellow in color is flowing out.  The more I scratch, the more is the itching.  Scab is forming after lesions.  Skin turns hard and starts shredding.  Mostly happening in Summer;  less serious in Winter.  Medical History: 1.  Diagnosis as eczema and epompholyx at the Dermatology Department of a local hospital.  Treatment is:  soaking in diluted Aluminum acetate solution, and topical use of Triamcinolone Acetonide Acetate and Miconazole Nitrate Neomycin Sulfate Cream.  No improvement after using them. 2. Soaking in salicylic acid solution by myself.  Effect is not obvious.  Have tried that 5 times. 3.  Topical use of Ketoconazole Ointment by myself.  Effect is not obvious. 4.  Diagnosis as epompholyx at a county level hospital. Treatment: Soaking in boric acid solution; oral use of Loratadine and XTR (Xiatare) tablets; Topical use of Urea ointment for one week.  After that change to another antifungal ointment which I don't recall the name. Also prescribed by the physician at the time is Compound Camphor Cream for use when itchy.  With all these, I don't feel itchy at all.  Even if I drink alcohol, the itchiness is still very minimal.  Unfortunately the problem comes back afte 6 months. 5. Soaking in Zuguang Powder+Pikangwang (A chinese herbal medicine containing Ketoconazole) every other night.  Yesterday was the 4 times.  The symptom is relieving a bit.  Skin is shredding, and becoming dry.  Topical use of Pikangwang is not very effective.  Each of the treatment and use of medications mentioned above is spaced at least 2 months apart.  I would be very grateful if you guys can offer any help.  The disease is so troublesome to me. I smoke, and not particularly fond of drinking alcohol (except when I accompany my boss on events).  I am fond of eating spicy food.  However, because of the problem on my hands, I have cut back most of it.  The pictures below are downloaded from the internet.  My situations are quite similar to them.  My skin is thicker, and the area of skin shredding is also bigger as well.  When the weather is warm, I can feel there are numerous blisters below the skin.  It is itchy, more so if I scratch it.
gold_texts: ["Eczema", "Should be eczema at the hands.", "The main thing is to avoid contact with alkaline solutions.  Otherwise problem would be coming back.", "Eczema is very stubborn.  Apart from the use of topical ointment, use of chinese herbal medicine to help conditioning of the body should be considered.", "Oral administration:  Pill prepared from Saposhnikovia divaricata + pill prepared from chinese angelica and Sophora flavescens (or Sophora flavescens tablets).  External use: Soaking in boiled water with 10g each of the following herbal medicines: Sophora flavescens, perfoliate knotweed, Bidens bipinnata, Vitiligo skin, hyssop,  and spina gleditsiae, After that topical use of Shufuda (A chinese over the counter herbal drug to help relieve problem of skin)"]

There is no use of protective gloves durig the process, and has direct contact with the hydrochloric acid.  Both hands are involved in the physical work, have direct contact with industrial grease.  Change of work place in March 2010.  Working in the office there, with occasional contact with metal.  Not sure it is caused by allergy with metal.  Symptoms:  Blisters on the sides of both palms.  Very itchy.  If broken after scratching, liquid of light yellow in color is flowing out.  The more I scratch, the more is the itching.  Scab is forming after lesions.  Skin turns hard and starts shredding.  Mostly happening in Summer;  less serious in Winter.  Medical History: 1.  Diagnosis as eczema and epompholyx at the Dermatology Department of a local hospital. query_text: Related to Eczema, pompholyx.  Request for diagnosis. Suffering eczema and epompholyx at hands from the year of 2010.  It is attacking me recurrently.  Very painful.  Hope that physicians from all places could take a look.  Following is the history:-  Work History:  Graduated in June, 2009.  Start working after that.  Work requires contact with  hydrochloric acid with low concentration inside a trench.  It is used to clean the oxidized surface of metal.  There is no use of protective gloves durig the process, and has direct contact with the hydrochloric acid.  Both hands are involved in the physical work, have direct contact with industrial grease.  Change of work place in March 2010.  Working in the office there, with occasional contact with metal.  Not sure it is caused by allergy with metal.  Symptoms:  Blisters on the sides of both palms.  Very itchy.  If broken after scratching, liquid of light yellow in color is flowing out.  The more I scratch, the more is the itching.  Scab is forming after lesions.  Skin turns hard and starts shredding.  Mostly happening in Summer;  less serious in Winter.  Medical History: 1.  Diagnosis as eczema and epompholyx at the Dermatology Department of a local hospital.  Treatment is:  soaking in diluted Aluminum acetate solution, and topical use of Triamcinolone Acetonide Acetate and Miconazole Nitrate Neomycin Sulfate Cream.  No improvement after using them. 2. Soaking in salicylic acid solution by myself.  Effect is not obvious.  Have tried that 5 times. 3.  Topical use of Ketoconazole Ointment by myself.  Effect is not obvious. 4.  Diagnosis as epompholyx at a county level hospital. Treatment: Soaking in boric acid solution; oral use of Loratadine and XTR (Xiatare) tablets; Topical use of Urea ointment for one week.  After that change to another antifungal ointment which I don't recall the name. Also prescribed by the physician at the time is Compound Camphor Cream for use when itchy.  With all these, I don't feel itchy at all.  Even if I drink alcohol, the itchiness is still very minimal.  Unfortunately the problem comes back afte 6 months. 5. Soaking in Zuguang Powder+Pikangwang (A chinese herbal medicine containing Ketoconazole) every other night.  Yesterday was the 4 times.  The symptom is relieving a bit.  Skin is shredding, and becoming dry.  Topical use of Pikangwang is not very effective.  Each of the treatment and use of medications mentioned above is spaced at least 2 months apart.  I would be very grateful if you guys can offer any help.  The disease is so troublesome to me. I smoke, and not particularly fond of drinking alcohol (except when I accompany my boss on events).  I am fond of eating spicy food.  However, because of the problem on my hands, I have cut back most of it.  The pictures below are downloaded from the internet.  My situations are quite similar to them.  My skin is thicker, and the area of skin shredding is also bigger as well.  When the weather is warm, I can feel there are numerous blisters below the skin.  It is itchy, more so if I scratch it.

gold_texts: ["Based on the symptoms you described and the picture, it should be keloid.  No need to think of syphilis stage 2 at this point in time.", "The appearance of the symptom of syphilis shouldn't come up so soon.  Should keep a close eye on that.  Monthly test should reveal if there is any problem."]

candidate: No, this is not a typical presentation of syphilis. Syphilis rashes usually appear on the trunk, chest, and back, but they are usually small, red, and raised. The presence of a keloid, which is a scar that is larger than normal and does not go away over time, suggests a different etiology. It is possible that the symptoms are related to an allergic reaction or a skin condition. It is important to consult a dermatologist for proper diagnosis and treatment.

It has a strong cell wall and a real nucleus.  It does not have chlorophyll and is heterotrophic.  Typically, it can be reproduced asexually and sexually, producing spores in various forms.  Based on the characteristics of its growth and difference in physical appearances, it can be separated as yeast, fungus and mushrooms.  Among them, 300 or so could cause diseases. Based on the locations of human body being invaded by fungus, clinically it can be separated into superficial fungal infection and deep fungal infection. Fungal enteritis is a deep fungal infection. Superficial fungal infection invades skin, hair and nail.  Deep fungal infection can invade body skin, mucosa, deep tissues and internal organs, and may result in disseminated hyperinfection of the whole body. query_text: Got my finger caught in the door, initially a small bruise, but now it's spreading. What should I do? I got my finger caught in a door, initially just a small bruise but now it's spread. What should I do? Any experts?
gold_texts: ["It is likely due to bleeding underneath the nail. Nail will likely fall off and need to completely regrow.  Given the extent of the injury would also recommend imaging if there is any pain to the finger to rule out underlying fracture."]

query_text: Help, I fell from a bike resulting in a wound like this. It has been two days, does it need stitches There was pus formation on the side. The wound is approximately 3-4mm deep. gold_texts: ["Closing the wound at this time risks infection. Continue to cleanse with soap and water and apply dry dressing. Does not appear to be a laceration amenable to suture repair.  If the wound is greater than 24 hours old is not appropriate for suture repair.  To promote wound healing, clean the wound thoroughly with warm soapy water.  You may apply topical antibiotic ointment and cover with a nonadherent dressing.  Dressing may be changed once daily and during dressing changes wound may be cleaned gently with warm soapy water.  Avoid any drying agents such as alcohol, hydrogen peroxide, or iodine to open skin.  Tetanus should be updated if tetanus is not up-to-date.  Once the wound is healed, to minimize scar formation avoid UV exposure and use of scar minimization creams such as Mederma"]

query_text: 请教各位专家，这是股癣吗？ 去两家医院看了，第一家说主要考虑还是真菌感染，第二家说是股癣。出现这样的疹子已经近1月，去医院之前先用了复方酮康唑药膏擦涂，似有好转，但后来疹子又增加。去医院看了以后，开始用联苯苄唑溶液，一直控制不住，从照片中可以看出疹子的面积在扩大。所以想请教专家，这是股癣吗，或者说是真菌感染吗？如果是，为什么用药控制不住，而且还在扩散和蔓延。表面好像没有什么皮损，不痛不痒。如果不是，那是什么？ 没有其他症状，不痛不痒，就是疹子越来越多，变大变多，用抗真菌药多久会有效？医生配了联苯苄唑喷剂，和盐酸特比萘芬乳膏，让我先喷，再用乳膏抹到患处，这样行吗

gold_texts: ["这两种药都是治疗股癣比较好的药了  要坚持使用2周以上或是皮损消退在继续使用1--2周以免复发", "去做个真菌刮片吧，感觉不象股癣", "觉得不像股藓", "湿疹？皮炎？", "印象：股癣 治疗：盐酸特比萘芬乳膏 外用（一定要足疗程）", "鉴别诊断 应与红癣、脂溢性皮炎、增殖性天疱疮、糜烂性银屑病等相鉴别。 5治疗 肥胖者应常洗澡并敷以扑粉等。 治疗同体癣。由于阴股部的皮肤较娇嫩，应注意勿用过于**的药水，以免**皮肤，一般用复方间苯二酚涂剂或1%益康唑或克霉唑霜，2%咪康唑、联苯苄唑、酮康唑、舍他糠唑、布替萘芬、特比奈芬霜也可用之。 口服灰黄霉素有一定疗效，但停药后易复发，不宜使用。氟康唑、伊曲康唑、特比奈芬等可短期使用。 病因 常由絮状表皮癣菌、须癣毛癣菌、红色毛癣菌等引起，有时，白色念珠菌也好侵犯腹股沟部位而呈红斑脱屑性斑片，其边缘可有丘疱疹。 本病在温热潮湿的季节容易发生，男性多汗者尤易发病。特殊工种如汽车司机、长期坐位者也易发病。 2临床表现 常发生于阴囊对侧的大腿皮肤，一侧或双侧，多呈环状或半环状斑片。初于股上部内侧出现小片红斑，其上有脱屑，并逐渐扩展而向四周蔓延，边界清楚，其上有丘疹、水疱、结痂。中央部位可自愈，有色素沉着或脱屑，历久则于局部皮肤发生浸润增厚呈苔藓化，常伴痒感。严重者常扩展波及股内侧、会阴或**周围，其下缘多清晰。有时尚可波及阴囊、**根部等处。 3诊断 根据临床表现、皮损形态及真菌学检查可确诊。 鉴别诊断 应与红癣、脂溢性皮炎、增殖性天疱疮、糜烂性银屑病等相鉴别。 5治疗 肥胖者应常洗澡并敷以扑粉等。 治疗同体癣。由于阴股部的皮肤较娇嫩，应注意勿用过于**的药水，以免**皮肤，一般用复方间苯二酚涂剂或1%益康唑或克霉唑霜，2%咪康唑、联苯苄唑、酮康唑、舍他糠唑、布替萘芬、特比奈芬霜也可用之。 口服灰黄霉素有一定疗效，但停药后易复发，不宜使用。氟康唑、伊曲康唑、特比奈芬等可短期使用。 真菌感染定义： 真菌（Fungus）是一种真核生物。最常见的真菌是各类蕈类，另外真菌也包括霉菌和酵母。现在已经发现了七万多种真菌，估计只是所有存在的一小半。大多真菌原先被分为动物或植物。 目前，真菌在分类学上已***为界，与动物界、植物界、原核生物界和原生生物界平行。真菌具有坚固的细胞壁和真正的细胞核，不含叶绿素，是异养性的，以寄生或腐生方式生存，典型者兼有有性生殖和无性生殖，产生各种形态的孢子。根据生长特性与形态差异，可将真菌简单分为酵母、真菌和蕈(蘑菇)。其中对人类有致病性的真菌约有300多个种类。除新型隐球菌和蕈外，医学上有意义的致病性真菌几乎都是霉菌。根据侵犯人体部位的不同，临床上将致病真菌分为浅部真菌和深部真菌。真菌性肠炎即属于深部真菌病。　浅部真菌(癣菌)仅侵犯皮肤、毛发和指(趾)甲，而深部真菌能侵犯人体皮肤、黏膜、深部组织和内脏，甚至引起全身播散性感染。深部真菌感染肠道即表现为真菌性肠炎，可***存在如婴儿念珠菌肠炎，或为全身性真菌感染的表现之一，如艾滋病并发播散性组织胞浆菌病", "做个真菌刮片", "不是股藓，股藓要痒，局部皮肤粘膜有白色粘膜样改变，一般不会出现疹子一样的病理，倒像是湿疹，做个细菌培养看看，如果是股藓可以配合制霉菌素口服。"]

query_text: 关于湿疹、汗疱疹，求诊断 手上湿疹、汗疱疹从10年至今，反复发作，痛苦啊，望各路医生给看看。以下是记录： 工作历程：09年6月毕业，之后工作，因工作需要接触低浓度盐酸，盐酸在槽内，用来洗去金属表面铁锈，操作过程无手套等保护，直接接触盐酸，同时双手参与体力活动，经常直接接触工业油污。10年3月后换单位，一直在办公室，偶尔接 触金属，不好判断是否金属过敏引起。 病症：双手手掌侧皮层内有泡，很痒，抓破后流出谈黄色液体，越抓越痒，皮损后结壳，皮肤变硬、脱屑。夏天多发、冬季减缓。 用药记录： 1、地级市皮肤医院诊断为湿疹、汗疱疹，用药醋酸铝粉末冲调后泡后，外用曲咪新乳膏，用后效果不明显。 2、自用药水杨酸浸泡，效果不明显，类似用药记录5。 3、自用复方酮康唑软膏，效果不明显。 4、县级市一医院诊断汗疱疹，用药硼酸浸泡、内服氯雷他定和百癣夏塔热片、外用尿素软膏一周后改用一种软膏，具体忘记了，好像是抗真菌的，同时医生开有复方樟脑乳膏，说痒的时候用。这一套下来之后不痒了，连喝酒之后都只有轻微的痒。不过半年后复发。 5、自用足光散+皮康王。足光散浸泡间隔一晚一次，昨天浸泡第4次。浸泡后痒的症状减轻了许多，手上脱皮、干燥，外用皮康王。效果感觉也不是很理想。 以上用药记录至少互相间隔2各月以上。各位大神，有没有好点的方法，跪求了。这病好烦人哎。 本人抽烟，不喜酒（陪领导的时候是没办法），爱吃各种**、辛辣，因为双手这样，也忌口了很多，哎。 以下图片是网上找到的，跟我手上差不多，我手上厚皮、脱屑面积比他大一些，天热的时候摸明显摸到皮下有很多泡，有点痒，越抓越痒的那种。
